rules.title: An IaC tool is present
rules.lesson: at team scale, infrastructure that exists only in a console is unreproducible; requires the mechanism (blocker), flags hand-applied changes (IAC-03) as deviation, not sin. docs/library SKIP via applies_to
rules.rationale: At team scale, infrastructure that exists only in a console is unreproducible and unauditable — manual provisioning is the named failure source. Research-narrowed: sources say "best practice", not "prohibited", so the rule requires the mechanism (blocker) and flags hand-applied changes as a deviation elsewhere.
rules.fix: Define the infrastructure in an IaC tool (Terraform/CDK/Pulumi/Serverless/CFN/Bicep) and commit it alongside the app.

rules.title: IaC state is remote/shared; local state never committed
rules.lesson: local state breaks team usage and committed state can leak secrets; HashiCorp states all three sub-claims verbatim. Team-tier floor is right — the cited complication is explicitly "in a team"
rules.rationale: Local state breaks team usage and committed state can leak secrets; HashiCorp states all three sub-claims (local fails teams, remote is the mechanism, never commit) verbatim. Non-Terraform IaC evaluates per-tool markers; CDK is tool-managed state (SKIP with detail).
rules.fix: Configure a remote backend (S3/HCP Terraform/etc.), delete any tracked state files, add *.tfstate* to .gitignore.

rules.title: IaC is applied by the pipeline, not by hand
rules.lesson: IaC humans apply from laptops reproduces the console problem with extra steps. Manual application is the flagged deviation (narrowed: "best practice", not "prohibited") — accepted via the ledger, not silently
rules.rationale: IaC that humans apply from laptops reproduces the console problem with extra steps: untracked who/what/when, no consistent workflow. Manual application is the flagged deviation (research narrowing), accepted consciously via the ledger. Heuristic line-scan.
rules.fix: Add an apply job to the pipeline (plan on PR, apply on merge); accepted manual application → pipeline jdg new --kind deviation --subject IAC-03 --reason "..." --review-by <date>.

check.pattern: terraform apply|pulumi up|cdk deploy|serverless deploy|aws cloudformation deploy

rules.title: Config drift is detected on a cadence
rules.lesson: drift is documented as expected and unpreventable — the failure is assuming it absent. Verify the detection mechanism exists at rest (scheduled plan/drift), or a drift-watch sign-off
rules.rationale: Drift is documented as expected and unpreventable — the failure is assuming it absent. The checker verifies the detection mechanism exists at rest (it can never see the cloud): a scheduled plan/drift workflow, or a dated sign-off recording a human reviews drift on a named cadence. Heuristic line-scan for the schedule+command.
rules.fix: Add a nightly cron workflow running plan/refresh-and-detect, failing (or filing) on diff; or record the drift-watch sign-off with a review_by.

rules.title: Per-environment IaC configuration is separated
rules.lesson: parity comes from replicating one definition across tiers with per-env differences in variables, not three divergent hand-copies. Scans for the documented Terraform per-env shapes
rules.rationale: Parity comes from replicating one definition across tiers with per-environment differences held in variables, not three divergent hand-copies. Research-narrowed: 12-factor is app-config-shaped; the Terraform per-environment patterns close the infra gap — the rule scans for exactly those shapes. Heuristic.
rules.fix: Restructure to one definition + per-env tfvars/workspaces/stacks; a genuinely single-environment shop SKIPs via profile.

rules.title: No plaintext secrets in IaC files; tfvars never committed
rules.lesson: secrets in IaC source land in state and plan files, and in git history are unrecoverable without rotation — a blocker is proportionate. Deterministic signature tier only + tfvars git-ignore; entropy heuristics stay baseline SEC (R3, overlap control)
rules.rationale: Secrets in IaC source land in state and plan files, and in git history they are unrecoverable without rotation — a blocker is proportionate. The check is the deterministic signature tier only (private-key blocks, cloud key shapes) plus the tfvars git-ignore rule HashiCorp points at; softer entropy heuristics stay with baseline’s SEC family.
rules.fix: Move secrets to a manager or protected CI variables; git rm --cached the tfvars, add *.tfvars to .gitignore, rotate anything that landed. False positive → pipeline jdg new --kind deviation --subject IAC-06 --reason "finding <id> is <test fixture|example>" --review-by <date>.
